taxonomy: Javieria rhopalorrhachis is a genus of brown algae, specifically a species of kelp found in the coastal waters of South Africa. This genus belongs to the family Sargassaceae. Javieria rhopalorrhachis is classified in the kingdom Chromista, phylum Ochrophyta, and class Phaeophyceae. The classification further categorizes it into the order Fucales and family Sargassaceae. Within this classification, Javieria rhopalorrhachis is one of the species found in the genus.
